It belongs to the genus Streptanthus, which is a group of flowering plants native to North America. Streptanthus amplexicaulis, also known as the clasping jewelflower or clasping brodiaea, is a species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.  There is limited research on the therapeutic properties of Streptanthus amplexicaulis, but it has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's roots and stems are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin irritations. However, further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. More research is necessary to fully understand the therapeutic potential of this plant species.
constituents: Streptanthus amplexicaulis contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids. These active constituents are thought to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The roots are typically harvested in the fall and dried for later use. In traditional medicine, Streptanthus amplexicaulis is often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
